name: PAC - Premature Atrial Contractions
identifyBy: Early P wave with different morphology from sinus P waves
QRS usually narrow - same as sinus QRS unless aberrant conduction
Usually followed by a non-compensatory pause (sinus node reset)
May occur in bigeminy (every other beat) or trigeminy
Aberrant PAC: wide QRS (RBBB morphology usually) - aberrant conduction due to early firing

measurements.pWaves: Ectopic P: different morphology, earlier than expected

management: Isolated PACs in structurally normal heart: reassure - benign
Reduce triggers: caffeine, alcohol, stress, sleep deprivation, hyperthyroidism
Frequent PACs (>1000/day): consider Holter, echocardiogram - trigger for AF in susceptible individuals
Beta-blockers or calcium channel blockers if symptomatic
PACs can trigger AF - if frequent with symptoms, cardiology review
pearls: PACs are the most common cardiac ectopic - present in virtually everyone at some point
Ectopic P wave may be hidden in the preceding T wave - distorting T wave shape
Non-compensatory pause: sinus node resets after PAC - RR interval containing PAC < 2x normal RR
Nonconducted PAC: early P wave with no QRS - may be confused with 2° AV block or SA block
Frequent PACs (>30/hour) with symptoms: consider catheter ablation at tertiary centre
